flags.description: Master toggle. Off = every login endpoint refuses with 503. Used for planned maintenance windows.
Off = POST /auth/register refuses with 503. Existing sessions continue; new-user provisioning halted.
Off = LoginAction never issues an MFA challenge. Sanctum PAT issued as if MFA weren't enrolled. RESERVED for catastrophic MFA-secret-KMS outage — two-person sign-off required to flip.
Off = refresh reuse is a soft warning instead of a hard revocation. Used for chaos testing / when investigating false-positive reuse signals (e.g. mobile client that stores tokens in two places).
Off = /auth/cross-app/* endpoints return 503. Existing exchanges succeed; new grants refused.
Off = /platform/auth/impersonate/* refuses with 503. Ops incident-response tool — flipped off when we suspect a compromise of platform-admin credentials.
Off = auth.jwt_verify middleware skips the deny-list check. Never used in production.
Off = the monthly RotateJwtSigningKeyJob is a no-op. Reserved for maintenance windows where key rotation must be manual.
Off = auth.detect_new_device middleware no-ops. Reserved for shared-IP environments (CGNAT deployments) generating too many false positives.
Off = PasswordChanged does NOT trigger cross-Application session revocation. Reserved for framework rollback — the invariant is documented in design.md §8. Never flip in production.
Off = auth.enforce_session_scope middleware skips the Application-scope check. Reserved for incident response during Application migrations.

Rollout gate for GenericRegistrationPolicy vs (future) DomainSpecificRegistrationPolicy overrides. Off for a tenant = falls back to a synchronous manual-approval flow instead of policy-based decision.
